name: CurvedMovement

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Move objects on curved paths in a given duration and tween easing function.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: TweenPathMovement

eventsFunctions.name: MoveTo
eventsFunctions.fullName: Move on path to a position
eventsFunctions.description: Move the object to a position by following a path.

eventsFunctions.name: MoveToAndGoBack
eventsFunctions.fullName: Move back and forth to a position
eventsFunctions.description: Move the object to a position by following a path and go back.

eventsFunctions.name: Move
eventsFunctions.description: Move the object by following a path.

eventsFunctions.name: MoveAndGoBack
eventsFunctions.description: Move the object by following a path and go back.

eventsFunctions.name: HasReachedAnEnd
eventsFunctions.description: Check if the object has reached one of the 2 ends of the path.

eventsFunctions.name: HasFinishedToMove
eventsFunctions.description: Check if the object has finished to move on the path.

eventsFunctions.name: MovementAngle
eventsFunctions.description: Return the angle of movement on its path.

eventsFunctions.name: DrawDebug
eventsFunctions.description: Draw the object trajectory.

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Move objects on curved paths at a given speed.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: SpeedPathMovement

eventsFunctions.name: SetPath
eventsFunctions.description: Change the path followed by an object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetPathTo
eventsFunctions.fullName: Follow a path to a position
eventsFunctions.description: Change the path followed by an object to reach a position.

eventsFunctions.name: PositionOnLoop
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the length between the trajectory origin and the current position without counting the loops.

eventsFunctions.name: Loop
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the number time the object loop the trajectory.

eventsFunctions.name: PositionOnPath
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the length between the trajectory origin and the current position counting the loops.

eventsFunctions.name: SetPositionOnPath
eventsFunctions.description: Change the length between the trajectory origin and the current position counting the loops.

eventsFunctions.name: HasReachedOrigin
eventsFunctions.description: Check if the object has reached the origin position of the path.

eventsFunctions.name: HasReachedTarget
eventsFunctions.description: Check if the object has reached the target position of the path.

eventsFunctions.name: HasReachedAnEnd
eventsFunctions.description: Check if the object has reached one of the 2 ends of the path.

eventsFunctions.name: CanMoveFurther
eventsFunctions.description: Check if the object can still move in the current direction.

eventsFunctions.name: MovementAngle
eventsFunctions.description: Return the angle of movement on its path.

eventsFunctions.name: Speed
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the speed of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetSpeed
eventsFunctions.description: Change the speed of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: AccelarateAt
eventsFunctions.description: Make an object accelerate until it reaches a given speed.

eventsFunctions.name: AccelarateDuring
eventsFunctions.description: Make an object accelerate to reaches a speed in a given amount of time.

eventsFunctions.name: DrawDebug
eventsFunctions.description: Draw the object trajectory.

eventsFunctions.name: PathOriginX
eventsFunctions.description: Return the path origin on X axis of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: PathOriginY
eventsFunctions.description: Return the path origin on Y axis of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: AddCubicCurve
eventsFunctions.description: Append a cubic Bezier curve at the end of the path.

eventsFunctions.name: AddSmoothCubicCurve
eventsFunctions.description: Append a cubic Bezier curve to the end of an object's path. The first control point is symmetrical to the last control point of the path.

eventsFunctions.name: AddLine
eventsFunctions.description: Append a line at the end of the path.

eventsFunctions.name: ClosePath
eventsFunctions.description: Append a line to close the path.

eventsFunctions.name: CreatePathFromSvg
eventsFunctions.description: Create a path from SVG commands, for instance "M 0,0 C 55,0 100,45 100,100". Commands are: M = Move, C = Curve, S = Smooth, L = Line. Lower case is for relative positions. The preferred way to build the commands is to use an external SVG editor like Inkscape.

eventsFunctions.name: ToSvg
eventsFunctions.description: Return the SVG commands of a path.

eventsFunctions.name: DeletePath
eventsFunctions.description: Delete a path from the memory.

eventsFunctions.name: AppendPath
eventsFunctions.description: Append a path to another path.

eventsFunctions.name: DuplicatedPath
eventsFunctions.description: Duplicate a path.

eventsFunctions.name: AppendRotatedPath
eventsFunctions.description: Append a path to another path. The appended path is rotated to have a smooth junction.

eventsFunctions.name: SpeedScaleY
eventsFunctions.description: Return the speed scale on Y axis. This is used to change the view point of a path (top-dwon or isometry).

eventsFunctions.name: SetSpeedScaleY
eventsFunctions.description: Change the speed scale on Y axis. This allows to change the view point of a path (top-dwon or isometry).

eventsFunctions.name: InvertPath
eventsFunctions.description: Invert a path, the end becomes the beginning.

eventsFunctions.name: ScalePath
eventsFunctions.description: Scale a path.

eventsFunctions.name: RotatePath
eventsFunctions.description: Rotate a path.

eventsFunctions.name: IsClosed
eventsFunctions.description: Check if a path is closed.

eventsFunctions.name: PathX
eventsFunctions.description: Return the position on X axis of the path for a given length.

eventsFunctions.name: PathY
eventsFunctions.description: Return the position on Y axis of the path for a given length.

eventsFunctions.name: PathAngle
eventsFunctions.description: Return the direction angle of the path for a given length (in degree).

eventsFunctions.name: PathLength
eventsFunctions.description: Return the length of the path.

eventsFunctions.name: PathEndX
eventsFunctions.description: Return the displacement on X axis of the path end.

eventsFunctions.name: PathEndY
eventsFunctions.description: Return the displacement on Y axis of the path end.

eventsFunctions.name: ElementCount
eventsFunctions.description: Return the number of lines or curves that make the path.

eventsFunctions.name: OriginX
eventsFunctions.description: Return the origin position on X axis of a curve.

eventsFunctions.name: OriginY
eventsFunctions.description: Return the origin position on Y axis of a curve.

eventsFunctions.name: FirstControlX
eventsFunctions.description: Return the first control point position on X axis of a curve.

eventsFunctions.name: FirstControlY
eventsFunctions.description: Return the first control point position on Y axis of a curve.

eventsFunctions.name: SecondControlX
eventsFunctions.description: Return the second control point position on X axis of a curve.

eventsFunctions.name: SecondControlY
eventsFunctions.description: Return the second control point position on Y axis of a curve.

eventsFunctions.name: TargetX
eventsFunctions.description: Return the target position on X axis of a curve.

eventsFunctions.name: TargetY
eventsFunctions.description: Return the target position on Y axis of a curve.

eventsFunctions.name: PathExists
eventsFunctions.description: Path exists.

description:
This extension allows to move objects on Bézier curve paths.
- Paths can be built dynamically or from predetermined paths in SVG format.
- A behavior moves objects in a given duration a bit like the Tween extension does.
- Another behavior moves objects using a speed and an acceleration.
- Expressions give access to the path trajectory values for more complex movements.

This extension can be used to:
- Move enemies that follow a pattern
- Animate decorations

A collection of [small examples](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://curved-movement) shows how to use this extension.

2 examples use it:
- a 3D racing game ([open the project online](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://3d-racing-game))
- a Zuma-like ([open the project online](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://smoothy))
